title: Procedure explanation — lumbar puncture consent
candidateInstructions: You are an FY2 on the neurology ward. Mr Stewart Price, a 45-year-old accountant, was admitted with a severe headache and fever. CT head is normal. Lumbar puncture has been recommended to rule out bacterial meningitis. He is anxious and needs you to explain the procedure and obtain consent.
examinerNotes: Lumbar puncture consent and explanation. Candidate must explain: position (lateral decubent or sitting), landmark (L3/L4 or L4/L5), purpose (CSF analysis for infection/SAH), risks (post-LP headache, infection, bleeding, back pain, very rarely nerve damage), benefits, contraindications (raised ICP, bleeding disorder, skin infection at site). Must not proceed without CT head (done and normal).

patient.name: Stewart Price
patient.presentingComplaint: Severe headache and fever — needs LP for meningitis
patient.reasonForAttendance: Admitted with severe headache and neck stiffness — being asked to have a lumbar puncture

examination.general: Ill-looking man, lying still with eyes shut
examination.neurological: GCS 15. Neck stiffness. Positive Kernig's sign. Photophobia.

patient.emotionalState: Very anxious about needles going near his spine. Worried about paralysis. Will ask 'can it paralyse me?' and 'what if I move?'
patient.communicationStyle: Anxious but intelligent. Will ask specific questions. Needs clear reassurance.
hiddenConcerns: Why can't you just treat me without the LP?
What happens if it comes back positive for meningitis?
doNotVolunteer: Fear of paralysis — only if doctor asks about concerns
Worry about pain — only if asked specifically

primaryDiagnosis: Possible bacterial meningitis — lumbar puncture required for diagnosis
differentialDiagnoses: SAH (CT negative does not fully exclude)

dataGathering.criterion: Checks patient understanding of why LP is needed
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Confirms patient knows about headache/meningitis investigation

dataGathering.criterion: Confirms CT head has been done and is normal
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Confirms no raised intracranial pressure before proceeding

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: To check for infection in the fluid around the brain and spinal cord
Position (curled up on side), local anaesthetic, needle in lower back L3/L4, small sample of fluid
Post-LP headache (15-25%), backache, bruising, local infection
Explains needle goes below spinal cord (L3/L4) — paralysis risk extremely low

clinicalManagement.criterion: Explains alternative — empirical antibiotics — and why LP is preferred
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Antibiotics can be started but LP is needed to identify organism and guide treatment

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Addresses anxiety, provides reassurance without dismissing concerns
Patient confirms they consent, right to refuse explained

references.source: NICE NG240: Meningitis (bacterial) and meningococcal disease
GMC: Consent — patients and doctors making decisions together

learningObjectives: Consent a patient for lumbar puncture
